Empires & Puzzles Torben Review: The Red Bear's Deadly Mark

Torben's 'Mark the Prey' mechanic was once the most feared skill in the arena. But with stats from a bygone era, can this Clash of Knights veteran still hunt in today's meta? We break down the numbers, the strategy, and the harsh reality of power creep.
Torben Review: Is The Lord of the Highlands Still King?
Torben: The Lord of the Highlands and His Shared Pain
Torben, the "Lord of the Highlands," marched into Empires & Puzzles during the Clash of Knights event in late 2023. A

, he brought a mechanic that had players trembling: a targeted damage link that turned an enemy's allies into their worst nightmare. While he doesn't have a costume to refresh his kit, his base form remains a unique tactical tool. He's essentially the red, slightly more complex cousin of the watery assassin Quintin.

Torben is a burly, bearded warrior clad in crimson plate armor, wielding a sword that looks like it could cleave a mountain in two. His bear-themed helm and fur accents scream "Northern durability," though his actual defense stat might disagree these days. He has that classic "don't mess with me or my porridge" look.

While he was balanced for 2023, in the current meta, he is fragile. He relies entirely on his Fighter class revive and careful team support to survive long enough to fire.
Torben’s skill, "Mark the Prey," is a masterpiece of single-target elimination, but it requires setup.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

Upon release, Torben was often compared to Quintin. While Quintin dealt 100% shared damage, Torben's ability to hit three targets and apply defense down gave him a different utility. He was a menace in Rush wars and a tactical nuke in raids.
He is too squishy for modern defenses. He will likely die before firing.

He hits three and drops defense, which is okay, but he's not a titan specialist.
His base stats are outdated; he needs every point of bulk to survive a single hit from a modern sniper.

. Torben's job isn't to deal damage with his sword; it's to apply the "Mark of Death." As long as he fires, he has done his job. Keeping him alive to fire that skill is priority #1.
The damage link mechanic is one of the few ways to reliably one-shot heroes with massive boosted health or high defense.
The Bear family bonus makes him annoying to debuff.
He turns your other AoE hitters into single-target nukes.
His biggest weakness. He is incredibly fragile in the current year.
His mark is a status ailment. If the enemy has a Monk who resists it, or a cleanser (like Vivica or Aeron) who fires immediately after, Torben does nothing.
He cannot win a 1v1. He needs allies to deal damage to the
enemies to hurt his marked target.
Specifically for dismantling teams with one problematic hero (like a reviver or a mega-tank).
Fun for manual play where you can time his skill perfectly.
Great for bosses with massive HP pools. Mark the boss, kill the minions, watch the boss die.
Fire your healer/buffer. Fire Torben at the most dangerous enemy. Fire Khufu/Gestalt. The AoE damage will hit everyone, and the marked target will take that damage
the shared damage, instantly vaporizing them.
If you don't have AoE, you can use tiles. Mark the target, then dump tiles into their neighbors. The damage transfer is often enough to finish them off without wasting another special.
Rigard, Vivica, etc., completely nullify his kit.
You can't mark the target you want if a taunter is active (unless you mark the taunter, which is also a valid strategy!).
Heroes like Garnet or Vanda prevent the mark from landing.

If you pulled Torben, congratulations! You have a "cheat code" for difficult bosses and raid teams. Level him up, but prioritize keeping him alive. Don't worry about his low direct damage; focus on the strategic elimination of key targets.

Torben is a luxury tool. He's not a daily driver anymore due to his stats, but keep him on your roster for war depth. He is an excellent "can opener" for specifically annoying defenses. Do not limit break him twice (LB2) unless you absolutely love his playstyle; the stats gain isn't worth the Alpha Aethers compared to a modern 2026 hero.
The community has always had a love-hate relationship with Torben.
On release, many called him a "Red Quintin," debating if the hit-3 vs hit-1 made him better or worse. The consensus was "different, but deadly."
Before release, his defense down was higher (50%), but it was nerfed to 44%. Players were relieved it wasn't "butchered."
One user noted, "He essentially does 500-650% damage to the target... The 200% damage to nearby is pretty poor." This holds true—don't rely on the splash damage.

Torben is like an old, reliable bear trap. He might be rusty (stat-wise) and a bit slower than the new high-tech gadgets, but if you step in him, you're just as dead. Use him to isolate and delete the biggest threats on the board, but protect him like the fragile tactician he has become.
A situational MVP who has aged gracefully in skill, but poorly in body.
A comprehensive review of Torben, the 5-star Fire hero from the Clash of Knights event. We analyze his stats, 'Mark the Prey' skill, and modern viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
